+# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)
+
+**Current stack:** 5 MSTest projects · MSTest 3.8.0 · Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0 · .NET 8 SDK  
+**CI:** Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task · TRX reporting · code coverage
+
+---
+
+## Strategy: Migrate to MSTest.Sdk
+
+The cleanest path is to adopt **MSTest.Sdk** (currently 4.1.0), which enables MTP
+by default and bundles TRX + Code Coverage extensions in its `Default` profile.
+This eliminates the three separate package references and most boilerplate.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 1 — Prepare the solution (no behavior change yet)
+
+### 1.1 Pin MSTest.Sdk version in `global.json`
+
+Create or update `global.json` at the repo root:
+
+```jsonc
+{
+  "sdk": {
+    "version": "8.0.400",          // keep your existing SDK pin
+    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
+  },
+  "msbuild-sdks": {
+    "MSTest.Sdk": "4.1.0"          // single place to update later
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### 1.2 Create / update `Directory.Build.props`
+
+Add shared MTP properties so they apply to every test project:
+
+```xml
+<Project>
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <!-- MTP opt-in (redundant with MSTest.Sdk but explicit) -->
+    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
+
+    <!-- Let 'dotnet test' talk to MTP via the VSTest bridge (.NET 8/9) -->
+    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
+
+    <!-- Show per-test failures on the console -->
+    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>true</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+</Project>
+```
+
+> If you already have a `Directory.Build.props`, merge these properties in.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 2 — Convert each test project to MSTest.Sdk
+
+For **each** of the 5 test `.csproj` files:
+
+### 2.1 Switch the SDK
+
+```diff
+- <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
++ <Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk">
+```
+
+(The version comes from `global.json`, so you don't repeat it here.)
+
+### 2.2 Remove now-redundant elements
+
+MSTest.Sdk sets these automatically — delete them:
+
+```diff
+- <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+- <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
+- <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
+- <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
+```
+
+### 2.3 Remove the old package references
+
+```diff
+- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
+- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
+- <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
+- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.Analyzers" Version="..." />   <!-- if present -->
+```
+
+The SDK's `Default` profile already brings in:
+
+| Package | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| MSTest.TestFramework | Test model |
+| MSTest.TestAdapter | Discovery/execution |
+| MSTest.Analyzers | Best-practice analyzers |
+| Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport | `--report-trx` |
+| Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage | `--coverage` |
+
+### 2.4 Keep non-MSTest package references
+
+Any other `<PackageReference>` items (Moq, FluentAssertions, etc.) stay as-is.
+
+### 2.5 Verify local build
+
+```bash
+dotnet restore
+dotnet build
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3 — Validate locally
+
+### 3.1 Run tests with `dotnet test`
+
+Because you're on .NET 8 SDK (< .NET 10), `dotnet test` talks to MTP through the
+VSTest bridge. MTP-specific arguments go **after `--`**:
+
+```bash
+# Basic run
+dotnet test --no-build
+
+# With TRX + code coverage
+dotnet test --no-build -- --report-trx --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
+```
+
+### 3.2 Run tests directly (optional, good smoke test)
+
+Each test project is now an executable:
+
+```bash
+dotnet run --project tests/MyTests/MyTests.csproj -- --list-tests
+dotnet run --project tests/MyTests/MyTests.csproj
+```
+
+### 3.3 Verify filters still work
+
+MSTest preserves the same `--filter` syntax on MTP:
+
+```bash
+dotnet test --filter "TestCategory=Unit" -- --report-trx
+```
+
+### 3.4 Verify `.runsettings` (if used)
+
+If you pass a `.runsettings` file, it still works:
+
+```bash
+dotnet test -- --settings my.runsettings --report-trx
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps pipeline
+
+### 4.1 Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
+
+```yaml
+# ── BEFORE ──
+- task: VSTest@3
+  inputs:
+    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
+    runSettingsFile: '$(Build.SourcesDirectory)/test.runsettings'   # if applicable
+    codeCoverageEnabled: true
+    publishRunAttachments: true
+
+# ── AFTER ──
+- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
+  displayName: 'Run tests (MTP)'
+  inputs:
+    command: 'test'
+    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'          # point at projects, not DLLs
+    arguments: >-
+      --configuration Release
+      --no-build
+      --
+      --report-trx
+      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
+      --coverage
+      --coverage-output-format cobertura
+```
+
+### 4.2 Publish test results
+
+TRX files are written to `--results-directory`. Publish them:
+
+```yaml
+- task: PublishTestResults@2
+  displayName: 'Publish test results'
+  condition: succeededOrFailed()
+  inputs:
+    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'           # TRX is the VSTest format
+    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
+    mergeTestResults: true
+```
+
+### 4.3 Publish code coverage
+
+```yaml
+- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
+  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
+  condition: succeededOrFailed()
+  inputs:
+    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.cobertura.xml'
+```
+
+> If you were merging coverage from multiple projects, MTP's Microsoft Code
+> Coverage extension auto-merges when tests run in the same `dotnet test`
+> invocation against a solution. If you run projects individually, use
+> `dotnet-coverage merge` afterwards.
+
+### 4.4 If you use `.runsettings`
+
+Add `--settings <path>` after the `--` separator:
+
+```yaml
+arguments: >-
+  --configuration Release
+  --no-build
+  --
+  --settings $(Build.SourcesDirectory)/test.runsettings
+  --report-trx
+  --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
+  --coverage
+  --coverage-output-format cobertura
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 5 — Handle behavioral differences
+
+### 5.1 Zero-test assemblies
+
+MTP **fails with exit code 8** when an assembly discovers zero tests (VSTest
+silently succeeds). If any project conditionally compiles out all tests, handle it:
+
+```xml
+<!-- In the affected project or Directory.Build.props -->
+<PropertyGroup>
+  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
+    $(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8
+  </TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
+</PropertyGroup>
+```
+
+### 5.2 Console encoding (edge case)
+
+MTP preserves `Console.InputEncoding`. If you have tests that spawn child
+processes with redirected stdin on Azure DevOps (codepage 65001), review the
+[encoding guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform#consoleinputencoding-preservation).
+
+### 5.3 Code coverage default change
+
+MTP's CodeCoverage extension sets `IncludeTestAssembly = false` by default
+(VSTest defaulted to `true`). If you relied on covering test-assembly code,
+set it explicitly in your coverage configuration.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 6 — IDE verification
+
+| IDE | MTP Support |
+|---|---|
+| Visual Studio ≥ 17.14 | Built-in |
+| Visual Studio < 17.14 | Update recommended |
+| VS Code + C# Dev Kit | Supported |
+| JetBrains Rider | Supported (recent versions) |
+
+Verify that Test Explorer discovers and runs tests after migration.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 7 — Clean up
+
+- [ ] Delete any leftover `vstest.console` references in scripts
+- [ ] Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from any shared props/targets if centralized
+- [ ] If using Central Package Management (`Directory.Packages.props`), remove
+      the old MSTest / Test.Sdk version entries
+- [ ] Commit and tag the migration
+
+---
+
+## Quick reference — command mapping
+
+| VSTest / old | MTP / new |
+|---|---|
+| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` |
+| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage` |
+| `--blame-crash` | `-- --crashdump` |
+| `--blame-hang` | `-- --hangdump` |
+| `--blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump-timeout 5m` |
+| `--filter "expr"` | `-- --filter "expr"` (same syntax for MSTest) |
+| `--settings file.runsettings` | `-- --settings file.runsettings` |
+| `--results-directory dir` | `-- --results-directory dir` |
+| `-t` / `--list-tests` | `-- --list-tests` |
+
+> **Remember:** the `--` separator is required on .NET 8/9 SDK. When you
+> upgrade to .NET 10 SDK and set `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }`
+> in `global.json`, the `--` separator is no longer needed.
+
+---
+
+## Risk assessment
+
+| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
+|---|---|---|
+| TRX output path differs from VSTest default | High | Explicit `--results-directory` in pipeline |
+| Third-party tooling doesn't recognize MTP yet | Low | TRX + Cobertura are standard formats |
+| Zero-test exit code breaks CI | Medium | `--ignore-exit-code 8` |
+| `.runsettings` entries not fully supported | Low | MSTest preserves runsettings support on MTP |
+| Coverage numbers differ slightly | Medium | `IncludeTestAssembly` default changed; verify |
+

**Approach:** Migrate to **MSTest.Sdk 4.1.0**, which enables MTP by default and bundles TRX + Code Coverage extensions.

**7 phases:**

1. **Prepare** — Add `MSTest.Sdk` version to `global.json`, set `EnableMSTestRunner` + `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in `Directory.Build.props`
2. **Convert projects** — Change each `.csproj` to `Sdk="MSTest.Sdk"`, remove old `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package refs
3. **Validate locally** — Run `dotnet test --no-build -- --report-trx --coverage` (note the `--` separator required on .NET 8 SDK)
4. **Update Azure DevOps** — Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`, add `PublishTestResults@2` and `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2` tasks
5. **Handle behavioral differences** — Zero-test exit code 8, `IncludeTestAssembly` default flip, console encoding
6. **IDE verification** — VS ≥ 17.14 required for built-in MTP support
7. **Clean up** — Remove leftover VSTest references

**Key gotcha:** On .NET 8/9 SDK, all MTP arguments must follow a `--` separator. This goes away on .NET 10 SDK.
